question.text: For how many assessment years can a business loss from exchange-traded derivatives trading be carried forward under the Income-tax Act, provided the return of income is filed on time?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The text states: "The unabsorbed loss can be carried forward up to 8 assessment years. It can be set off only against the business income in the subsequent years. It is important to note that the assessee is entitled to carry forward the business loss provided the return of income is filed on or before the due date." **The Trap:** 4 years and 5 years are limits applicable under other tax loss carry-forward provisions (e.g., speculation losses in certain contexts). For business income, the carry-forward is 8 years. **Cross-Exam Context:** Business loss from derivatives CANNOT be set off against salary income but CAN be set off against other business/professional income in the same year. Carry-forward to subsequent years is only against business income. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** 8 assessment years for carry-forward. Must file ITR on time to preserve this right — late filing forfeits the carry-forward benefit.
